sources.notes: Count subsegments where some value appears between k and floor(len/2) times.
Official editorial derives the answer by subtracting segments with frequency below k and uses the fact that two such high-frequency values cannot coexist.

annotation.summary: Count subarrays in which at least one value occurs at least k times but not more than half of the subarray length.
annotation.constraints: Total n is up to 2e5, so the number of invalid/valid windows must be counted with moving pointers.
annotation.core_idea: A value satisfying cnt*2<=len and cnt>=k is unique enough that windows can be handled by its kth occurrence. Count all subarrays with some value appearing at least k times, then subtract subarrays where the only such heavy value occupies more than half the length. Occurrence position lists and two pointers maintain the minimal left/right extension around kth occurrences.
annotation.complexity: O(n) to O(n log n) per test case depending on occurrence-list implementation.

solution_variants.name: occurrence-list-two-pointers
solution_variants.summary: Group positions by value, count windows around kth occurrences, and use two pointers to remove windows where that value is a strict majority.

tags.evidence: The solution scans occurrence positions with moving boundaries to count valid and over-majority windows.
The main counting step separates windows with enough occurrences from those where that same value is too dominant.
Positions are grouped by compressed value or hash-map buckets before running per-value scans.
